subtopic: Microsoft Azure Cloud Design Patterns catalog
note: These 43 patterns are the complete catalog as listed by Microsoft. Each entry below is copied verbatim from the source's summary column. The catalog tags each pattern with one or more Azure Well-Architected Framework pillars: Reliability, Security, Cost Optimization, Operational Excellence, Performance Efficiency.

patterns.name: Ambassador
patterns.summary: Create helper services that send network requests on behalf of a consumer service or application.

patterns.name: Anti-Corruption Layer
patterns.summary: Implement a façade or adapter layer between a modern application and a legacy system.

patterns.name: Asynchronous Request-Reply
patterns.summary: Decouple back-end processing from a front-end host. Useful when back-end processing must be asynchronous but the front end requires a clear and timely response.

patterns.name: Backends for Frontends
patterns.summary: Create separate backend services for specific frontend applications or interfaces.

patterns.name: Bulkhead
patterns.summary: Isolate elements of an application into pools so that if one fails, the others continue to function.

patterns.name: Cache-Aside
patterns.summary: Load data on demand into a cache from a data store.

patterns.name: Choreography
patterns.summary: Let individual services decide when and how a business operation is processed, instead of depending on a central orchestrator.

patterns.name: Circuit Breaker
patterns.summary: Handle faults that might take a variable amount of time to fix when an application connects to a remote service or resource.

patterns.name: Claim Check
patterns.summary: Split a large message into a claim check and a payload to avoid overwhelming a message bus.

patterns.name: Compensating Transaction
patterns.summary: Undo the work performed by a sequence of steps that collectively form an eventually consistent operation.

patterns.name: Competing Consumers
patterns.summary: Enable multiple concurrent consumers to process messages they receive on the same messaging channel.

patterns.name: Compute Resource Consolidation
patterns.summary: Consolidate multiple tasks or operations into a single computational unit.

patterns.name: CQRS
patterns.summary: Separate operations that read data from those that update data by using distinct interfaces.

patterns.name: Deployment Stamps
patterns.summary: Deploy multiple independent copies of application components, including data stores.

patterns.name: Event Sourcing
patterns.summary: Use an append-only store to record a full series of events that describe actions taken on data in a domain.

patterns.name: External Configuration Store
patterns.summary: Move configuration information out of an application deployment package to a centralized location.

patterns.name: Federated Identity
patterns.summary: Delegate authentication to an external identity provider.

patterns.name: Gatekeeper
patterns.summary: Protect applications and services by using a dedicated host instance to validate and sanitize requests before forwarding them to private back ends.

patterns.name: Gateway Aggregation
patterns.summary: Use a gateway to aggregate multiple individual requests into a single request.

patterns.name: Gateway Offloading
patterns.summary: Offload shared or specialized service functionality to a gateway proxy.

patterns.name: Gateway Routing
patterns.summary: Route requests to multiple services by using a single endpoint.

patterns.name: Geode
patterns.summary: Deploy back-end services across geographically distributed nodes. Each node can handle client requests from any region.

patterns.name: Health Endpoint Monitoring
patterns.summary: Implement functional checks in an application that external tools can access through exposed endpoints at regular intervals.

patterns.name: Index Table
patterns.summary: Create indexes over the fields in data stores that queries frequently reference.

patterns.name: Leader Election
patterns.summary: Coordinate actions in a distributed application by electing one instance as the leader. The leader manages a collection of collaborating task instances.

patterns.name: Materialized View
patterns.summary: Generate prepopulated views over the data in one or more data stores when the data is poorly formatted for required query operations.

patterns.name: Messaging Bridge
patterns.summary: Build an intermediary to enable communication between messaging systems that are otherwise incompatible.

patterns.name: Pipes and Filters
patterns.summary: Break down a task that performs complex processing into a series of separate elements that can be reused.

patterns.name: Priority Queue
patterns.summary: Prioritize requests sent to services so that requests with a higher priority are processed more quickly.

patterns.name: Publisher-Subscriber
patterns.summary: Enable an application to announce events to multiple consumers asynchronously, without coupling senders to receivers.

patterns.name: Quarantine
patterns.summary: Ensure that external assets meet a team-agreed quality level before the workload consumes them.

patterns.name: Queue-Based Load Leveling
patterns.summary: Use a queue that creates a buffer between a task and a service to smooth intermittent heavy loads.

patterns.name: Rate Limiting
patterns.summary: Avoid or minimize throttling errors by controlling the consumption of resources.

patterns.name: Retry
patterns.summary: Enable applications to handle anticipated temporary failures by retrying failed operations.

patterns.name: Saga
patterns.summary: Manage data consistency across microservices in distributed transaction scenarios.

patterns.name: Scheduler Agent Supervisor
patterns.summary: Coordinate a set of actions across distributed services and resources.

patterns.name: Sequential Convoy
patterns.summary: Process a set of related messages in a defined order without blocking other message groups.

patterns.name: Sharding
patterns.summary: Divide a data store into a set of horizontal partitions or shards.

patterns.name: Sidecar
patterns.summary: Deploy components into a separate process or container to provide isolation and encapsulation.

patterns.name: Static Content Hosting
patterns.summary: Deploy static content to a cloud-based storage service for direct client delivery.

patterns.name: Strangler Fig
patterns.summary: Incrementally migrate a legacy system by gradually replacing pieces of functionality with new applications and services.

patterns.name: Throttling
patterns.summary: Control the consumption of resources from applications, tenants, or services.

patterns.name: Valet Key
patterns.summary: Use a token or key to provide clients with restricted, direct access to a specific resource or service.

common_pairings_from_source: Retry + Circuit Breaker — application retries transient faults but stops retrying when a fault persists
Queue-Based Load Leveling + Competing Consumers — buffer load and then scale the processing of that load
Gateway Routing + Gateway Aggregation + Gateway Offloading — layered behind a single gateway endpoint
Saga + Compensating Transaction — maintain data consistency across services when a distributed operation fails partway through
